findings.message: Using clear-text protocols is security-sensitive. The FTP protocol transmits data in clear text, making it vulnerable to eavesdropping and man-in-the-middle attacks. Use FTPS (FTP over SSL/TLS) or SFTP (SSH File Transfer Protocol) instead for secure file transfers.

Using clear-text protocols is security-sensitive. The FTP protocol transmits data in clear text, making it vulnerable to eavesdropping and man-in-the-middle attacks. Use FTPS (FTP over SSL/TLS) or SFTP (SSH File Transfer Protocol) instead for secure file transfers.

Using clear-text protocols is security-sensitive. The FTP protocol transmits data in clear text, making it vulnerable to eavesdropping and man-in-the-middle attacks. Use FTPS (FTP over SSL/TLS) or SFTP (SSH File Transfer Protocol) instead for secure file transfers.

Using clear-text protocols is security-sensitive. The FTP protocol transmits data in clear text, making it vulnerable to eavesdropping and man-in-the-middle attacks. Use FTPS (FTP over SSL/TLS) or SFTP (SSH File Transfer Protocol) instead for secure file transfers.

Using clear-text protocols is security-sensitive. The FTP protocol transmits data in clear text, making it vulnerable to eavesdropping and man-in-the-middle attacks. Use FTPS (FTP over SSL/TLS) or SFTP (SSH File Transfer Protocol) instead for secure file transfers.
Using clear-text protocols is security-sensitive. Telnet transmits all data, including passwords, in clear text. Use SSH instead for secure remote access.
Using clear-text protocols is security-sensitive. Telnet transmits all data, including passwords, in clear text. Use SSH instead for secure remote access.
Using clear-text protocols is security-sensitive. Telnet transmits all data, including passwords, in clear text. Use SSH instead for secure remote access.
Using clear-text protocols is security-sensitive. HTTP transmits data in clear text without encryption. Use HTTPS instead to protect data in transit from eavesdropping and tampering.
